title: Metric-Native Refoundation: rebuild the metric layer on the proven registry-quadtree topology skeleton
thesis: Every measured defect in the conforming mesher is a METRIC error, not a topology error: the GATE-B sliver regression, the helix-shear parallelograms, the cross-crest CDT needles, and the SFB@1 non-manifold (an order-of-operations bug amplified by anisotropic snap boxes) all stem from decisions made in raw axis-aligned (u,t) space while the true geometry lives under a warp-composed Riemannian metric. Meanwhile the topology layer â€” periodic 2:1-balanced quadtree + edge-local vertex registry + per-cell constrained CDT + exact analytic GPU evaluation â€” is verbatim the literature's answer to by-construction watertightness (restricted-quadtree crack rules, edge-local shared-vertex functions). The principled re-architecture is therefore NOT a new pipeline: it replaces the scattered axis-aligned scalar approximations (worst-point global uBias, isotropic scalar sizing, raw-(u,t) cdt2d, hardcoded legacy templates, greedy unguaranteed ear-clip) with ONE mathematically coherent object â€” a warp-composed pullback metric field M(u,t) = J_w^T G(w(u,t)) J_w covering the u-warp, t-warp, and helix shear â€” consumed by every decision: cell sizing and aspect (tensor sizing + percentile-calibrated B + log-ratio gradation), template/diagonal selection (Klincsek O(n^3) DP for the certified per-cell max-min 3D angle, replacing the greedy ear-clip), feature-cell fill (cdt2d in per-cell M^(1/2)-stretched coordinates where constant-metric Delaunay optimality holds exactly), and crest-neighborhood point placement (in-metric clearance snapping + crest-aligned metric-normal Steiners with accept/reject). The shared-edge registry contract is the unchanged watertightness invariant throughout; the one place a deeper change may be forced (SpiralRidges shear ceiling) gets an instrumented decision with a provable criterion, not a guess.

stages.name: Stage 0 â€” PullbackMetricField + ear-clip safety proofs (pure addition, production byte-identical)
stages.what: Build the single source of metric truth: a PullbackMetric module that takes the base sampler's first fundamental form G(u,t) (via the existing firstFundamentalForm, SurfaceMetricTensor.ts:115-126) and the active domain-warp chain chosen in ParametricExportComputer (u-warp phi, t-warp psi, helix shear s = turns/k) and returns the composed metric by closed-form Jacobian transform: helix u_f = phi0(u) - s*t gives E' = E*phi0'^2, F' = phi0'*(F - s*E), G' = G - 2sF + s^2*E; u-warp scales E' = phi'^2*E, F' = phi'*F; t-warp scales G' = psi'^2*G, F' = psi'*F. Composition must mirror the EXACT application guards at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2418-2481 (helix only when u-warp is identity; t-warp walls-only; helix on both walls; caps rigid), pinned by a unit test comparing composed-metric values against finite differences of the actually-applied warp chain per style class. Any wrapper sampler forwards gridResolution() so metricStepsForSampler never falls back to the noise-amplifying DEFAULT_H=1e-4. Second deliverable: a brute-force property test of earClipMaxMinAngle over rectangle+mids weakly-convex polygons under anisotropic metrics targeting its two unguarded corners (zero-area final-3 emission at QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:188-190; no-valid-ear break at :181 stranding boundary sub-edges = a real crack), plus guards (area-check the final 3 and re-split; prefer ears whose removal keeps a non-collinear remainder). The guarded path stays dead in production (leaf.efg still unpopulated), so output is byte-identical.
stages.watertightArgument: No production code path consumes the new module yet, and the ear-clip guards live inside a path that is provably unreachable until leaf.efg is populated (shapedTemplate returns false without efg, QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:101). The registry, quadtree, and all templates are untouched; the 20-style default output is hash-identical by construction.
stages.measuredGate: 318+ unit tests green; new property tests pass over exhaustive rectangle+mids polygons (4-8 vertices) under metrics with aspect up to 16:1 â€” zero zero-area emissions, zero stranded boundary sub-edges, exactly k-2 triangles; composed-metric unit test matches finite-difference Jacobians to 1e-9 per style class; full 20-style default export hash-compare: byte-identical.

stages.name: Stage 1 â€” Activate in-metric connectivity: warp-composed leaf.efg + certified Klincsek DP templates (flag-gated, explicit re-baseline)
stages.what: Populate the dead path correctly on the first try: retain the metric sampler as a field in PeriodicBalancedQuadtree (the constructor currently discards it after refine; only this.steps survives at :204) and lazily compute leaf.efg in leaves() (PBQ:555-565) â€” NOT in leafOfCell, which sits on the hot neighbors() path â€” from the Stage-0 PullbackMetric at the cell center ((iu+0.5)/2^eUL, (it+0.5)/2^level). Thread an efgMetric option through ConformingWallOptions -> buildConformingWall -> buildWallMeshAtScale -> buildQuadtreeAtScale -> the quadtree constructor, and per-wall fields through AssemblyWallOptions in WatertightAssembly (BOTH walls get the composed metric â€” the helix shears both; inner walls of CDT styles are feature-free and route through these same templates). ParametricExportComputer builds the composed metric right after the warp choices (:2297-2316). Warp-composed from day one because the u-warp and t-warp are ALSO post-triangulation vertex remaps â€” plain-sampler efg would optimize the WRONG metric exactly near the pinned creases of Gothic/GeometricStar/BambooSegments/DragonScales/CelticTriquetra, i.e. most of the slivered set. Simultaneously replace the greedy ear loop inside the shaped path with Klincsek's O(n^3) dynamic program maximizing the minimum 3D-metric angle (law-of-cosines on metricLen2) â€” the exact optimum over all triangulations of the fixed cell polygon, at n<=12 trivially cheap, removing the greedy's no-guarantee status and its strand-failure mode. Land behind a dev flag (__pfMetricTemplates) with a full side-by-side matrix before defaulting; this stage is the explicit re-baseline event for all B>0 styles.
stages.watertightArgument: Pure interior connectivity choice over the IDENTICAL registry-built boundary polygon: the shaped/DP path emits only existing caller-supplied boundary indices (no vertexIndex calls, no Steiner points, no moved vertices), so every shared cell edge keeps its exact vertex set and both neighbours still derive bit-identical points from the edge-local registry functions â€” the T-junction-free guarantee is template-independent. The plain-quad diagonal flip keeps QSCALE tie-quantization (exact ties resolve to legacy SW->NE) for determinism; seam handling (per-leaf wrapsSeam, u=1->u=0 index remap) is untouched. Vertex/triangle counts DROP on transition cells (legacy centroid omitted, k -> k-2 triangles) â€” a connectivity-and-count change only, never a shared-edge change; Stage-0 guards exclude the weak-convexity crack corners.
stages.measuredGate: triangleQualityDistribution + sliver counts on the 8 plain-path slivered styles (ArtDeco 2107, Crystalline 2027, DragonScales 2683, BasketWeave 1584, GeometricStar 166, BambooSegments 291, CelticTriquetra 1221, SpiralRidges 479): collective reduction >= 80%, SpiralRidges allowed a partial drop pending Stage 5; diagnoseTopoQuality bnd=0/nonMan=0/orient=0 on 20/20 at default AND the four dimension extremes; crestBandTriangleQuality non-regression on Gothic/GeometricStar (the warp-composition correctness probe); B=0 clean styles verified byte-identical OR each diff traced to a >2:1-aspect cell crossing the ANISO gate; build time within +5% of the 7-36s envelope; 318+ tests green.
stages.effort: medium (1-1.5 weeks including the full re-measure/re-baseline protocol)

stages.name: Stage 2 â€” Metric-true, robust feature-cell CDT (SFB@1 non-manifold at root; Voronoi)
stages.what: Measurement first, per the project rule: a single-cell repro harness dumps the failing SFB@1 cell (boundary, interior points, constraints) pre-cdt2d and counts pre-flip negative-area triangles, confirming the hypothesis that PASS-A planarization followed by PASS-B endpoint merges (dedupSide/interior-weld/resolve under the 8:1 anisotropic snap box at B=3, cornerSnapU = cornerSnap/2^B vs cornerSnapT = cornerSnap) re-introduces crossing constraints that cdt2d folds and the silent winding flip masks as duplicated directed edges. Then five changes: (a) re-run planarization over the RESOLVED index-space cellConstraints in PASS B immediately before triangulateConstrainedCell (FeatureConformingTriangulator.ts between :814 and :822), splitting at strictly-interior Steiners â€” the same cross-cell-safe argument as PASS A; (b) count pre-flip negative-area triangles in ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:81-87 and treat >0 as a per-cell error with a DETERMINISTIC local fallback (retry without cross-line merges, then drop the newest constraint) instead of silently flipping; (c) forbid dedupSide/interior-weld merges that join endpoints of DIFFERENT feature lines unless planarity rechecks clean; (d) run cdt2d in per-cell M^(1/2)(cell-center)-stretched coordinates â€” a constant metric is an affine pullback of Euclidean, so Delaunay regains exact max-min-angle optimality IN the metric (= true 3D angles), and the stretch separates the near-coincident points the anisotropic snap box manufactures; pin resolve()'s order-dependent tie-break to deterministic min-qk; (e) mirror Stage 1's templates into the feature path's hardcoded plain-cell branch (FCT:687-707): shorter-metric-diagonal for 0-split cells, Klincsek DP for split cells. This removes the need to cap B at 2 â€” the topology guard becomes structural, keeping the B=3 serration gain.
stages.watertightArgument: The cell boundary polygon (4 corners + transition mids + grid-line-registry feature points after dedupSide) is never altered: the M^(1/2) stretch is a per-cell-internal coordinate change used only for connectivity decisions â€” emitted triangles reference the same (u,t) vertices, and collinearity of on-edge points is affine-invariant so the closed boundary-loop constraints survive the stretch; all new planarization Steiners are strictly interior with the existing STEINER_MIN_EDGE_DIST = 2e-6 clearance (> 2x WELD_TAU, > one QSCALE quantum); the cross-line merge restriction is a function of registry data only, hence cross-cell symmetric â€” both neighbours still derive bit-identical edge points. No post-hoc repair is added anywhere; the negative-area fallback re-triangulates the SAME cell with fewer merges, never touching shared vertices.
stages.measuredGate: Repro harness first: failing SFB@1 cell shows pre-flip negative-area count > 0 at B=3 and 0 at B=2 (hypothesis confirmed before any fix lands). Then: SFB@1 at auto-B3 nonMan 3 -> 0 (duplicated total=4 edges gone) with sub-15-degree share <= the 10.4% B=2 baseline; Voronoi nonMan 1 -> 0 and slivers 2076 -> large measured drop; featuresDropped = 0 on Hive/CelticKnot/Gyroid/Voronoi/SFB (diagnoseFeatures); all gates via the faithful e2e export-fidelity path (real WebGPU exports â€” unit tests overstate seam cracks); build-time envelope respected on feature-dense styles.

stages.name: Stage 3 â€” Tensor sizing + percentile-calibrated aspect; retire worst-point GATE B as the sole anisotropy mechanism
stages.what: Upgrade MetricSizingField from one scalar target per node (principalCurvatureMax + sagitta law) to per-node directional targets (hU, hT) from directional normal curvature along u and along t, each graded with a per-axis log-ratio Lipschitz bound (BAMG-style metric gradation â€” the standard guard against needles where anisotropy changes fast, and the textbook explanation of the ArtDeco grading-transition sliver history at WatertightAssembly.ts:42-49). shouldRefine consumes per-axis physical extents against per-axis targets (splits remain the square 4-way â€” per-axis SPLITTING with 2-axis balance is explicitly out of scope as measured-broken: the eUL cascade caused the documented ArtDeco timeout). Recalibrate computeUBias GATE B from the single worst-point maxURatio (the proven B=3-overshoot mechanism: one steep petal flank re-meshes the whole wall) to a high percentile (e.g. P90) of the u-dominant ratio over the 192x192 lattice, with a relief-gated FLOOR preserving the banked serration win (SFB crestRms -38% depended on GATE B; the floor is whatever the crest-band instrument demands, measured not assumed). Stage 1's shaped templates absorb the residual local anisotropy a percentile-B leaves; gradation bounds the jumps. One B per wall pair stays (ring-match invariant). The crease biasFreeU mechanism and buildCreaseRefineLines are kept untouched as the crest-coverage guarantee.
stages.watertightArgument: Sizing and aspect changes alter only WHICH dyadic leaves exist, never how vertices are derived: every vertex remains a dyadic grid-line point deduped by quantized key (QSCALE exact for dyadic coords), side mid-vertex existence remains an edge-local probe of the shared leaf set, and 2:1 balance still runs after all refine decisions (constructor order refine -> pin -> balance). The eUL = level + B bijection that makes a global B watertight is preserved because B stays global per wall pair; the pinned boundary rings stay at nRing via the existing pinBoundaryLevel shift. No vertex positions move; conformity falls out of the same four invariants as today.
stages.measuredGate: Full matrix, 20 styles x (default + short-wide H40/R150 + tall-narrow + high-flare + no-drain): sliver = 0 on all plain-path styles (the 9-style regression closed in combination with Stage 1); crease coverage via diagnoseFeatures >= 0.9 on Gothic (the 6aafe5b regression sentinel); crestRms/serration non-regression on SFB@1 against the dense decoupled reference (__pfReferenceDenseRes) â€” the GATE-B floor proves itself here; wallChordError <= 0.03mm on smooth regions everywhere; build time within the current envelope (7-36s default, no feature-dense blowup); 318+ tests green including updated ComputeUBias tests.

stages.name: Stage 4 â€” Crest-band quality floor: in-metric clearance snap + crest-aligned Steiners (+ offset-ribbon escalation)
stages.what: Kill the diagonal/helical crest serration with the two registry-compatible mechanisms production meshers use, in escalation order. (a) In-metric clearance snapping (Bern-Eppstein-Gilbert warped-quadtree / CGAL protecting-ball discipline): when a crest edge-crossing or crest interior sample falls within epsilon measured in M of a registry grid vertex (or of another constraint point on the same edge), snap the CREST point onto the grid vertex via an edge-local deterministic rule â€” a function of (global curve, grid line) only, so both neighbours derive the identical snapped point; never move registry vertices. This removes the near-duplicate vertex pairs that the 1e-9-tolerance non-clean-edge dedup leaves and that CDT fans into needles. (b) Crest-aligned interior Steiner insertion: for feature cells whose post-CDT minimum metric angle is below the gate, insert interior (u,t) points along the metric-NORMAL offsets of the crest chord (the tangent is b-a from the cell's data.constraints â€” zero new plumbing) at metric-unit spacing with the 2e-6 side clearance, positions evaluated analytically on the true surface, each insertion ACCEPTED ONLY IF the measured 3D min angle improves (Bossen-Heckbert accept/reject) â€” reusing the proven-safe refineCellInterior replay wiring (FCT:836-842) with a metric-true kernel replacing the flat-affine kernel that was measured harmful. (c) Escalation, only on a measured residual: compute 1-2 global OFFSET curves per crest (crest +/- delta_k along the metric normal in the parameter domain) and feed them through the SAME FeatureConformingTriangulator constraint machinery â€” the region between crest and offset becomes a structured ribbon of fine-perpendicular/coarse-along elements (gmsh BoundaryLayer / LayTracks translated to global-curve form), with offset cusp/self-intersection trimming for high-curvature crests.
stages.watertightArgument: (a) The snap rule is an interior-independent, edge-local function: the snapped position depends only on the global crest curve and the global grid line, so both cells sharing the edge compute the identical point â€” exactly the registry contract; grid vertices themselves are never moved, so all other shared edges are unaffected. (b) Interior Steiners obey the established constrained-cell contract: boundary polygon and constraint endpoints replayed unchanged, insertions strictly interior with >= 2e-6 clearance from every side (above WELD_TAU and the QSCALE quantum), so global dedup/weld backstops can never alias them with shared vertices; the accept/reject wrapper means a bad candidate is simply not inserted â€” there is no failure mode that touches topology. (c) Offset curves are deterministic global curves entering through the SAME registry/clipping discipline as crests (per-edge-LINE crossings derived bit-identically by both neighbours); no new topology mechanism is introduced.
stages.measuredGate: crestBandTriangleQuality on SFB@1, HexagonalHive, CelticKnot, Gyroid, Voronoi rises to within noise of each style's bulk triangleQualityDistribution (no sub-15-degree cluster along inserted crests); e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs min-angle tint render shows zero red clusters on crests (visual confirmation requirement from the definition of done); featuresDropped = 0 and crest polylines survive as exact mesh edge chains; true crestRms (dense decoupled reference) holds at ~0.08-0.13mm CAD tolerance; escalation (c) triggers only if (a)+(b) leave a measured gap, and its gate is the same instrument plus the build-time envelope on feature-dense styles.
stages.effort: large (2-3 weeks including the ribbon escalation if triggered)

stages.name: Stage 5 â€” SpiralRidges ceiling instrumentation + lattice decision
stages.what: Resolve the one defect class where the fixed cell skeleton has a PROVABLE quality ceiling. With Stage 1's warp-composed efg live, instrument the analytic per-cell ceiling: a helix-sheared cell's post-warp corner angle is 90deg - atan(s*sqrt(E/G)) â€” independent of cell aspect (du, dt cancel) â€” and since a corner angle partitions among the triangles incident at that immutable boundary vertex, NO triangulation of the cell (any diagonals, any interior Steiners) can have min angle above it. Emit a per-cell ceiling map for SpiralRidges and overlay it on the measured post-Stage-1 min angles. Outcome A (residuals sit above their cells' ceilings): they were template-suboptimal and Stage 1 already fixed them â€” close the defect. Outcome B (residual clusters AT their ceiling, i.e. bands where s*sqrt(E/G) > tan(75deg) ~= 3.73): the floor is irreducible in the current build domain; decide between (i) accepting a quantified, localized, documented floor (printability judgment: count and area of ceiling-bound triangles), or (ii) the d'Azevedo metric-aligned lattice: build the SpiralRidges quadtree in sheared coordinates v = u - s*t so post-warp cells are rectangles â€” the registry contract survives verbatim because the sheared grid lines are still global analytic curves (edge-local crossing functions) and the shear commutes with u -> u+1 periodicity â€” at the real cost that crest columns become diagonal lines in v and must be CDT-inserted and handled by the Stage 4 machinery instead of riding grid-aligned vertex columns. The choice is made on the ceiling map, not on intuition.
stages.watertightArgument: The instrumentation half is measurement-only (no mesh change). If option (ii) triggers, the invariant is RESTATED, not replaced: every vertex on a shared cell edge remains an interior-independent, edge-local function â€” now of globally-defined SHEARED grid lines (v = const, t = const), which are analytic curves both neighbours evaluate identically; the quadtree, registry keys, 2:1 balance, and templates run unchanged in (v,t) coordinates; periodicity holds because the shear is equivariant under v -> v+1; vertices still GPU-evaluate exactly on the analytic surface at u = v + s*t. Crest insertion in (ii) reuses the Stage 2/4 constrained machinery with its existing watertight contract.
stages.measuredGate: Ceiling map produced for SpiralRidges (and archived as an instrument for any future warped style); SpiralRidges sliver count 479 -> ~0 if not ceiling-bound, else every residual sub-15-degree triangle is certified ceiling-bound (measured angle within tolerance of its cell's analytic ceiling) and the bound population/area is quantified in the export validation summary; if option (ii) is taken, its gate is sliver = 0 + crestBandTriangleQuality at bulk + featuresDropped = 0 + watertight 20/20 under the sheared lattice, with all other styles byte-identical (the lattice change is style-scoped).
stages.effort: small for instrumentation (2-3 days); medium (1-1.5 weeks) if the sheared lattice triggers

stages.name: Stage 6 â€” Cutover slot-in + CI fidelity gates (Phase 1 of the existing cutover plan)
stages.what: Slot the metric-native mesher into the existing export-pipeline cutover plan (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-export-pipeline-cutover-plan.md) as its Phase-1 meshing payload â€” the flag source, validationSummary unification, format fix, and LOD/decimation phases belong to that plan and are NOT redesigned here. This stage contributes the two pieces the definition of done demands of the meshing side: (1) a headless CI gate that runs the REAL e2e export-fidelity matrix (20 styles x default + short-wide H40/R150 + tall-narrow + high-flare + no-drain) and asserts sliver=0, boundaryEdges=0, nonManifold=0, orientationMismatches=0, featuresDropped=0, crestBandTriangleQuality >= bulk-parity threshold, and wallChordError <= 0.03mm on smooth regions â€” using the faithful WebGPU path, since the UV-only unit tests are known to overstate seam cracks; (2) the new per-style output hashes archived as the post-re-baseline byte-identity reference so the project's hard constraint is enforceable again going forward. Only after this matrix is green twice consecutively does the cutover plan flip DEFAULT_FEATURE_FLAGS.conformingMesher / wire the no-dialog buttons â€” critical because the conforming validation gate hard-fails (throws, no file) on sliverCount > 0 where legacy shipped defective-but-downloadable meshes.
files: C:/Users/patij212/Downloads/PotFoundry-Lite-v2.0/potfoundry-web/src/renderers/webgpu/parametric/contracts.ts (flag flip executed by the cutover plan, gated on this stage)
stages.watertightArgument: No meshing change â€” gate-only. Watertightness is what this stage MEASURES rather than argues: the CI matrix is the standing enforcement of the registry contract's output (bnd=0/nonMan=0 by construction, now continuously verified on real exports), and the archived hashes restore the byte-identity discipline that Stages 1-5 necessarily interrupted.
stages.measuredGate: CI matrix green on two consecutive runs across all 20 styles x 5 dimension sets with every definition-of-done counter at zero and both quality floors (crest band, chord error) met; a default-button export (post cutover-plan wiring) completes without the strict validator throwing on any style; total matrix runtime documented and within the CI budget (feature-dense high-fidelity styles batched or run at export-default profile per the cutover plan's perf phase).
stages.effort: small-medium (3-5 days, excluding the cutover plan's own phases)

coverage.spiralRidgesShear: Addressed in two steps. Stage 1 composes the helix pullback (E'=E*phi0'^2, F'=phi0'(F-sE), G'=G-2sF+s^2E) into leaf.efg so the certified DP template picks the shear-adapted diagonal â€” triangles align with the helix; expect a large drop from 479 slivers. Stage 5 then computes the PROVABLE per-cell ceiling: a sheared cell's post-warp corner angle is 90deg - atan(s*sqrt(E/G)), aspect-independent, and no triangulation (diagonals OR interior Steiners) can beat the corner angle since it partitions among triangles incident at the immutable boundary vertex. If the ceiling map shows bands where s*sqrt(E/G) > tan(75deg) ~= 3.73, the residual is irreducible in the current build domain and the design forces a measured choice: accept a quantified localized floor, or the d'Azevedo sheared-lattice build (mesh in v = u - s*t; the registry survives because sheared grid lines are still global analytic curves and the shear commutes with u-periodicity) at the cost of crest columns becoming CDT-inserted diagonals handled by Stage 4. Honest: not claimed fully fixed until the ceiling map is measured.
coverage.sfbCdtNonManifold: Addressed at ROOT CAUSE in Stage 2, explicitly rejecting the B=2 cap (symptom treatment that forfeits the proven B=3 serration gain and leaves the merge-after-planarize bug armed for any future high-B style). Per the project's measurement-first rule, a single-cell repro harness first confirms the hypothesis (PASS-A planarization precedes the PASS-B endpoint merges; the 8:1 anisotropic snap box at B=3 merges endpoints of different crest chords, re-crossing constraints; cdt2d folds; the silent negative-area winding flip at ConstrainedCellTriangulator.ts:84-86 converts the fold into the observed 3-duplicated/total-4 edge signature). Fix: re-planarize after endpoint resolution, hard per-cell negative-area detection with deterministic fallback, cross-feature-line merge restriction, and cdt2d in metric-stretched coordinates so the near-coincident points never form. Gate: SFB@1 at auto-B3 nonMan 3 -> 0 with quality at or better than B=2's 10.4% sub-15-degree residual, then improved further by Stage 4.
coverage.nineSliveredStyles: Fully targeted, split by path. The 8 plain-path styles (ArtDeco 2107, Crystalline 2027, DragonScales 2683, BasketWeave 1584, GeometricStar 166, BambooSegments 291, CelticTriquetra 1221, SpiralRidges 479) get Stage 1 (warp-composed efg + certified DP templates â€” the warp-pinned styles among them REQUIRE composition; a plain-sampler efg would optimize the wrong metric exactly near their pinned creases) plus Stage 3 (percentile-damped B replacing the worst-point max that over-corrects t-dominant/isotropic bands into 2^B:1 slivers, with log-ratio gradation bounding the anisotropy jumps that manufacture grading-transition slivers â€” the documented ArtDeco-B=3 mechanism). Voronoi (2076 slivers + nonMan=1) routes through the CDT path and gets Stage 2 (robust metric-true CDT + the plain-branch template port, since Stage 1 alone provably cannot reach feature-bearing outer walls) and Stage 4. Gate: sliver=0 at default dims AND the four dimension extremes for all nine.
coverage.smoothBelowPrinterRes: Preserved by construction, not re-solved: vertex positions remain exact analytic GPU evaluations of (u,t) at every stage (no stage manufactures positions outside parameter space â€” the flat-plane back-map class of error is structurally excluded), and Stage 3's tensor sizing can only refine more where directional curvature demands it. The shipped profile-driven result (smooth-surface facets 0.082 -> 0.021mm at 'high', below the ~0.03mm printer resolution, commit 9ca22fe) is gated for non-regression at EVERY stage via wallChordError with the decoupled dense reference (__pfReferenceDenseRes) on smooth regions of all 20 styles, and permanently enforced by the Stage 6 CI matrix.
coverage.highReliefResiduals: Partially addressed â€” honest. Crystalline is in the 9-style sliver set and is covered by Stages 1+3. FourierBloom's high-relief min-angle residual should improve under Stage 3's directional-curvature tensor sizing plus Stage 1's in-metric templates, but the known weak point is the per-leaf CONSTANT cell-center metric misrepresenting sub-cell relief (the same curved-surface error class that killed refineCellInterior). Mitigations: connectivity-only choices cannot corrupt geometry (worst case = a suboptimal diagonal, never a manufactured sliver), and Stage 4(b)'s accept-only-if-measured-3D-min-angle-improves Steiner kernel extends to plain high-relief cells as the escalation. If the post-Stage-3 measurement still shows a FourierBloom residual, it is documented as a bounded, quantified floor â€” this design does not guarantee its elimination and says so.
coverage.diagonalCrestSerration: Addressed by Stage 4 on the Stage 2 foundation, in escalation order with a measured trigger for each step: (a) in-metric clearance snapping of crest samples onto nearby registry grid vertices (Bern-Eppstein-Gilbert / CGAL protecting-ball discipline, an edge-local rule so both neighbours derive bit-identical points) kills the near-duplicate thin wedges at the source; (b) crest-aligned interior Steiners along metric-normal offsets of the crest chord (tangent = b-a from data.constraints, zero new plumbing) at metric-unit spacing, analytically evaluated on the true surface, accept/reject on the measured 3D min angle â€” reusing the proven-safe refineCellInterior replay wiring with a metric-true kernel replacing the flat-affine one that was measured harmful; (c) only if a residual is measured: global offset curves per crest through the SAME registry-proven constraint machinery, forming a structured fine-perpendicular/coarse-along ribbon (gmsh BoundaryLayer translated to global-curve form). Gate: crestBandTriangleQuality at bulk parity on SFB@1/Hive/CelticKnot/Gyroid/Voronoi and zero red clusters in the e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs min-angle tint.

risks: Re-baseline blast radius: Stage 1 flips ALL leaves of every B>0 wall (including the feature-free INNER walls of CDT styles, which share the outer wall's uBias) onto shaped templates, and transition cells lose their legacy centroid vertex (k -> k-2 triangles) â€” byte-identity is gone for most styles at once. Mandatory protocol: land behind a flag, run the full 20-style x extremes side-by-side matrix, re-baseline explicitly per the project's hard constraint before defaulting; Stage 6 re-archives hashes so the discipline is enforceable afterward.

earClipMaxMinAngle has two unguarded weak-convexity corners (zero-area final-3 emission at QuadtreeTriangulator.ts:188-190; no-valid-ear break at :181 leaving boundary sub-edges with no incident triangle â€” a REAL crack class on these weakly-convex corner+mid+corner polygons). These MUST be property-tested and guarded in Stage 0 while the path is still dead code, before Stage 1 activates it; the Klincsek DP replacement structurally removes the greedy strand-failure but the guards remain as invariant checks.

Constant per-leaf cell-center metric on sub-cell relief is the same error class that made refineCellInterior manufacture slivers. The mitigation is structural (connectivity-only choices cannot move vertices â€” worst case is a suboptimal diagonal â€” and every Steiner insertion is accept/reject on the MEASURED 3D min angle), but crest-band and high-relief gains must be measured per stage, never assumed; the gates are written accordingly.

Stage 3's percentile-damped B could un-bank the shipped serration win (relief-gated GATE B drove SFB crestRms -38%, commit 6602c1c, and B=3 is part of the SFB quality story). The stage keeps a relief-gated floor and its gate includes crestRms/serration non-regression against the dense decoupled reference â€” if percentile-B and serration conflict on a style, B stays high and the shaped templates absorb the t-dominant-band over-correction instead.

Stage 2 changes interior Steiner sets on every inserted-feature style (Hive, CelticKnot, Gyroid, Voronoi, SFB), and strict negative-area handling may surface latent fold-overs that currently pass topology checks by luck. The fallback must be deterministic and LOCAL (retry without cross-line merges, then drop the newest constraint), never a throw mid-export; featuresDropped=0 is in the stage gate to catch over-aggressive fallbacks.

Warp-composition guard drift: the composed metric must mirror the EXACT application semantics at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2418-2481 (helix only when the u-warp is identity; t-warp walls-only via surfaceId<1.5; helix on BOTH walls with caps rigid). Stage 0 pins this with a finite-difference-vs-closed-form unit test per style class; silent divergence would mis-meter every downstream decision precisely in the crest neighbourhoods the design targets.

SpiralRidges may be ceiling-bound in bands (provably irreducible: the post-warp corner angle 90deg - atan(s*sqrt(E/G)) partitions among incident triangles regardless of interior Steiners). The sheared-lattice escape sacrifices crest-edge grid alignment (crests become CDT-inserted diagonals dependent on Stage 4 machinery); take it only if the Stage 5 ceiling map proves necessity â€” otherwise accept and document the quantified floor rather than trading a measured small defect for a structural unknown.

Performance: per-leaf efg is 4 bilinear CPU samples (well under 1% of 7-36s builds; populate only in leaves(), never on the hot leafOfCell/neighbors path) and Klincsek is O(n^3) on n<=12 polygons (negligible), but Stage 2's re-planarize pass and Stage 4's optional ribbons add constrained edges on feature-dense styles already running 60-236s at high fidelity â€” every stage gate carries a build-time envelope check, and the Stage-1 triangle-count drop slightly mis-calibrates the TRIS_PER_LEAF=2 budget search, which must be re-verified.

Invariant couplings that must not break silently: one shared B per wall pair (ring-match â€” assembly throws otherwise) constrains Stage 3 to per-pair recalibration; the crease biasFreeU/buildCreaseRefineLines coverage mechanism must remain the only source of crest refinement footprints (any new near-crest refinement bypassing it can re-strip crest t-rows at high B, the 6aafe5b regression mechanism); resolve()'s enumeration-order tie-break must be pinned to deterministic min-qk when Stage 2 touches that region.

Cutover ordering risk: the conforming validation gate hard-fails (throws, no file) on sliverCount>0 while the shipping legacy path delivers defective-but-downloadable meshes. Stage 6 must come strictly after Stages 1-4 demonstrate sliver=0 on the full matrix, or flipping the default converts today's silent quality defects into user-visible export failures on 9/20 styles.
whyThisOverAlternatives: The honest survey verdict is that every rip-and-replace candidate forfeits a non-negotiable constraint the current skeleton already guarantees: dual contouring puts QEF vertices off the analytic surface and merges the pot's thin double wall at coarse cells; field-aligned remeshers (Instant Meshes/QuadriFlow) relax vertices off-surface and require the forbidden validate-and-repair pass; subdivision surfaces swap the true displaced revolve for a limit surface and re-import the crest-aligned-cage problem unsolved; GPU-tessellation crack rules are literally the registry the project already has. The only principled rival â€” global curve-first Riemannian advancing-front meshing (BLSURF/Tristano-Owen lineage) â€” attacks the same root cause this design attacks, but costs months of robustness engineering (front collisions, metric discontinuities at crest junctions, no off-the-shelf periodic constrained CDT) to re-reach a 20/20-watertight baseline that ~25k lines and 318 tests already hold, and trades the registry's local by-construction guarantee for a global one with new failure modes. The decisive observation is that the defect ledger is one-sided: boundaryEdges=0 and nonManifoldâ‰ˆ0 everywhere (the single nonMan has an identified order-of-operations cause), while ALL remaining defects â€” 9-style slivers, helix shear, crest serration, high-relief residuals â€” are metric errors. Re-architecting the metric layer into one warp-composed pullback object consumed by sizing, aspect, templates, CDT, and point placement is exactly the mathematics BAMG/gmsh/Mmg ship in production, costs weeks not months, keeps every shipped win (watertightness, exact-on-surface vertices, periodic u, sub-printer-resolution smoothness) intact by construction, upgrades the one unguaranteed heuristic in the plan (greedy ear-clip) to a certified optimum (Klincsek DP) for free, and holds curve-first global meshing as the documented fallback with a concrete measured trigger â€” the Stage 5 ceiling map â€” instead of a hunch. Where the design honestly cannot promise zero (SpiralRidges ceiling bands, FourierBloom sub-cell relief), it says so and ships the instrument that quantifies the floor rather than a claim.

title: Surgical evolution: certify, arm, and extend the in-metric triangulation machinery already inside the conforming mesher
thesis: Every open defect class is a METRIC problem, not a topology problem: the registry/quadtree watertight machinery is sound and is exactly the literature-standard crack-free construction, but every triangulation CHOICE inside it (diagonal selection, transition fans, CDT fill, snap tolerances, uBias) is blind to the true 3D metric â€” and for warped styles blind to the post-triangulation domain remap on top of that. The smallest path to DONE is therefore: (1) certify the existing dead metric-aware template path against its two real weak-convexity hazards, (2) arm it with a WARP-COMPOSED first fundamental form (correcting the handoff: not just the helix â€” the u-warp and t-warp are also post-triangulation, so plain-sampler efg is wrong on Gothic/GeometricStar/BambooSegments-class styles too, and plain efg must never ship alone), (3) mirror the same templates into the CDT path's plain cells which the handoff's Step A provably cannot reach, (4) fix the CDT non-manifold at root (re-planarize AFTER PASS-B endpoint resolution, make the silent negative-area winding flip a fail-loud counter) instead of capping B, (5) add in-metric connectivity flips + crest-aligned interior Steiners inside feature cells for the diagonal-crest sawtooth, (6) a conditional, measurement-triggered escape for SpiralRidges' provable parallelogram corner-angle ceiling, and (7) extremes hardening + a real-export CI gate that hands a green matrix to the existing cutover plan's Phase 1. Every stage is interior-only or observability-only with respect to the shared-edge registry contract, independently landable, and gated on the existing fidelity instruments.

stages.name: Stage 1 â€” Certify the dead path before arming it (production byte-identical)
stages.what: Harden the shaped-template machinery while it is still provably unreachable (efg never populated in production). (a) Replace the greedy ear order in earClipMaxMinAngle (QT:149-191) with Klincsek's O(n^3) max-min-weight dynamic program over the cell polygon, weighted by triMinAngle3D under efg â€” at n=4-12 this is trivially cheap and yields the CERTIFIED optimum over all triangulations of the fixed polygon, eliminating both weak-convexity hazards by construction (the DP only considers complete triangulations: exactly k-2 positive-area triangles, every boundary sub-edge covered exactly once). If the minimal diff is preferred instead: keep the greedy but add (i) an ear filter rejecting ears whose removal leaves an all-collinear remainder, and (ii) an area check + re-split on the final-3 emission (QT:188-190) â€” either option closes the break-at-:181 crack hazard and the zero-area final-3 hazard. (b) Add a brute-force property test enumerating rectangle+mid polygons (all 16 mid subsets x anisotropy E/G up to 2^8 x F up to near-degenerate, plus sheared metrics) asserting: k-2 triangles, all CCW positive area, every boundary sub-edge has exactly one incident triangle, no emitted index outside the input set. (c) Fix the ANISO_ASPECT_GATE comment/code mismatch (QT:63-71 documents a hypotenuse-inclusive sqrt(3) isotropy score; cellAspect3D at :84-91 actually scores sqrt(3)/2 for isotropic, so the gate crosses at ~2:1 physical aspect) â€” keep the code behavior, correct the comment, record the intended-gate question as a measured follow-up if Stage 2 leaves B=0 residuals.
stages.watertightArgument: Double-walled. (1) The path remains dead in production throughout this stage â€” leaf.efg is still never populated (verified: PBQ:555-565 and :584-593 omit it; only test fixtures set it) â€” so production output is byte-identical by unreachability. (2) The hardened triangulator itself is interior-only: it emits exclusively over caller-supplied boundary indices, never calls vertexIndex, never adds/moves/subdivides a vertex; the boundary polygon (corners + registry-union mids) is byte-identical to the legacy template's, so every shared cell edge keeps its exact vertex set. The Klincsek DP strengthens the contract: a complete triangulation of the polygon is guaranteed, so every boundary sub-edge gets exactly one incident triangle inside the cell â€” the one corner case where the greedy could have produced a real crack (all-collinear remainder, QT:181) becomes structurally impossible.
stages.measuredGate: New property test green over the exhaustive small-polygon enumeration (zero crack / zero-area cases); full 318+ unit suite green; byte-identity spot-check: hash-compare one default-dims export per style against the pre-stage baseline (must be identical for all 20 â€” the path is dead).

stages.name: Stage 2 â€” Arm the metric: warp-composed efg threaded to both walls, shaped templates live on plain-path walls
stages.what: Populate leaf.efg from a WARP-COMPOSED sampler, never the plain one (correction to the handoff's Step A/B split â€” verified: the u-warp PEC:2418-2422 and t-warp PEC:2434-2441 are post-triangulation vertex remaps just like the helix PEC:2466-2481, so plain efg is metrically wrong exactly in the crest neighbourhoods being targeted). Changes: (1) PeriodicBalancedQuadtree.ts â€” add optional efgMetric sampler to the constructor opts, RETAIN it as a field (the constructor currently discards the metric after refine; only this.steps survives at :204), and compute efg = firstFundamentalForm(efgMetric, cellCenter, steps) lazily in leaves() ONLY (:555-565) â€” never in leafOfCell (:584-593), which sits on the hot neighbors() path; the budget binary-search calls only leafCount(), so zero added search cost. (2) ConformingWall.ts â€” thread efgSampler through ConformingWallOptions -> buildConformingWall -> buildWallMeshAtScale -> buildQuadtreeAtScale into the quadtree opts. (3) WatertightAssembly.ts â€” per-wall efg sampler fields in AssemblyWallOptions, forwarded at the buildConformingWall call sites (:421-429); BOTH walls need the composed metric (the helix shears both, verified at PEC:2470). (4) ParametricExportComputer.ts â€” after the warp choices (:2297-2316), build wrapper samplers position(u,t)=base.position(uMap(u,t), tMap(t)) mirroring the EXACT application guards (helix only when u-warp is identity; t-warp walls only; caps not meshed by the quadtree, so no cap efg) and forwarding gridResolution() so metricStepsForSampler does not fall back to the noise-amplifying DEFAULT_H=1e-4; verify GpuSurfaceSampler.position tolerates the un-folded u that applyHelixWarp emits BEFORE composing. Identity warps compose to the base sampler, so unwarped styles get plain efg automatically â€” handoff Steps A and B land as one coherent increment. (5) Full 20-style re-baseline with the re-measure protocol: shapedTemplate fires on ALL leaves of every B>0 wall (including the feature-free INNER walls of CDT styles, which share the outer uBias per WA:417/429) and on >~2:1-aspect cells of B=0 walls.
stages.watertightArgument: efg changes only WHICH template fires per leaf; both templates (legacy fan and Stage-1-certified shaped) consume the identical registry-built boundary polygon, and the shaped path is interior-only (no vertexIndex calls; the legacy centroid at QT:502/528 is simply omitted, so vertex COUNT drops but no shared-edge vertex set changes). The eUL=level+B bijection, 2:1 balance, QSCALE dyadic dedup, and seam remap are template-independent and untouched. The efg sampler influences zero vertex positions â€” positions remain exact GPU evaluations of registry-derived (u,t) coordinates. The re-baseline is a connectivity/count change on shaped cells, never a conformity change; this is the same watertight argument the in-code earClip contract states (QT:138-148), now backed by the Stage 1 property test.
stages.measuredGate: Full 20-style default-dims matrix via diagnoseTopoQuality + triangleQualityDistribution + summarizeConformingValidation: (a) bnd=0, nonMan unchanged-or-better, orient=0 everywhere (hard gate); (b) sliver counts on the plain-path slivered styles (ArtDeco 2107, Crystalline 2027, DragonScales 2683, GeometricStar 166, BambooSegments 291, SpiralRidges 479, plus inner walls of CDT styles) drop to 0 â€” first VERIFYING each style's routing at ConformingWall.ts:447, since the read-only agents disagree on which of the 9 are plain-path; (c) B=0 smooth styles byte-identical OR explained solely by >2:1-aspect cells, with wallChordError (decoupled __pfReferenceDenseRes reference) unchanged; (d) build time within 1.2x current per style; (e) crestBandTriangleQuality recorded as the Stage 5/6 baseline.
stages.effort: medium (2-4 days including the re-baseline protocol)

stages.name: Stage 3 â€” Mirror the shaped templates into the CDT path's plain cells
stages.what: The handoff's Step A provably cannot reach feature-bearing outer walls: triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures never reads leaf.efg, and its non-feature 'plain template cell' branch (FCT:687-707, verified first-hand) is a hardcoded copy of the LEGACY templates (fixed SW->NE diagonal for 0-split cells, unconditional centre-fan with vertexIndex(um,tm) for any split). Export Efg, metricLen2, cellAspect3D, shapedTemplate, and the Stage-1-certified triangulator from QuadtreeTriangulator.ts (currently module-private), and replace FCT:687-707 with the same shaped choice: metric shorter-diagonal for 0-split cells, certified max-min-3D-angle polygon triangulation for transition cells, gated by shapedTemplate(leaf.efg, sizeU, sizeT, uBias) with legacy fall-through otherwise. This covers the large plain-cell population inside feature walls (BasketWeave, CelticTriquetra, Voronoi, Hive, CelticKnot, SFB outer walls) â€” the feature CELLS themselves are Stages 4/5.
stages.watertightArgument: Identical to Stage 2's: the plain-cell boundary polygon in the CDT path (corners + transition mids, FCT:689-697) is exactly the registry-derived vertex set both neighbours agree on; the shaped replacement emits only over those existing indices and omits the interior centroid. Cells that PASS B promotes to feature cells (because a neighbour registered edge points) are untouched by this stage â€” the isFeature branch (FCT:684-685) and everything below it is unchanged, so the feature-cell edge-vertex union contract is unaffected. Legacy fall-through preserves byte-identity wherever shapedTemplate does not fire.
stages.measuredGate: Sliver counts (aspect>100 via summarizeConformingValidation + sub-15-degree population via triangleQualityDistribution) on the CDT styles' outer walls drop measurably (BasketWeave 1584, CelticTriquetra 1221, Voronoi 2076 â€” expect partial movement, since feature cells remain); diagnoseTopoQuality bnd=0 / nonMan unchanged-or-better / orient=0 on all 20; diagnoseFeatures featureDrop=0 unchanged; 318+ suite green with re-baselined snapshots.

stages.name: Stage 4 â€” Make the CDT fill fail loud, then fix the planarize-before-merge ordering (root cause of the SFB@1 B=3 non-manifold)
stages.what: Measurement-first, then the root fix â€” NOT the B=2 cap (which forfeits the proven B=3 serration gain and leaves the latent bug armed). Phase 4a (observability, zero behavior change): add a dev-gated pre-flip negative-area counter in ConstrainedCellTriangulator (:81-87 currently flips area<0 triangles to CCW SILENTLY â€” the masking point that converts a folded, self-overlapping cdt2d patch into duplicated directed edges); add a one-cell dump harness serializing the failing SFB@1 cell's boundary/interior/constraints pre-cdt2d. Run SFB@1 at auto-B=3 to confirm the hypothesis: PASS-A planarizeConstraints (FCT:634, verified) runs BEFORE the PASS-B endpoint merges (dedupSide :743-760, interior weld :777-791, nearest-fallback resolve :798-813), so the 8:1-anisotropic snap box (cornerSnapU=cornerSnap/2^B vs cornerSnapT=cornerSnap, FCT:263-264) can merge endpoints of DIFFERENT dense crest chords and re-introduce interior-crossing constraints that cdt2d cannot handle. Phase 4b (fix, conditional on 4a confirming): (1) re-run a segSegCross planarization pass over the RESOLVED index-space cellConstraints in PASS B, immediately before triangulateConstrainedCell (between FCT:814-821 and :822), splitting at strictly-interior Steiner points â€” same cross-cell-safe argument as the existing PASS-A planarize (:201-207); (2) promote the negative-area flip to a counted per-cell error with a deterministic local fallback (retry the cell without cross-feature-line merges, then without the newest constraint split) instead of a silent flip; (3) pin resolve()'s order-dependent tie-break (d<=bestD keeps the LAST index, FCT:808-811) to deterministic min-qk; (4) optionally forbid dedupSide/interior-weld merges joining endpoints of DIFFERENT feature lines unless planarity is re-checked. Keep the one-line hasFeatures B-cap (WA:151) documented as containment-of-last-resort only if 4b fails its gate.
stages.watertightArgument: Every move is interior or observability-only with respect to the registry contract. The re-planarize pass splits constraints only at STRICTLY interior intersection points (never on a cell side), so the boundary polygon and the registry-union edge-vertex sets both neighbours read are bit-identical before and after; constraint ENDPOINTS on the boundary are never moved. The negative-area counter changes nothing; its fallback alters only INTERIOR connectivity/Steiner sets of one cell. The tie-break pinning makes intra-cell constraint routing deterministic without touching any shared vertex. Cross-cell symmetry of dedupSide (registry union + min-qk canonical) is preserved because no merge rule affecting boundary points changes â€” only merges between interior endpoints of different feature lines are restricted.
stages.measuredGate: 4a: the dump harness reproduces SFB@1's 3-duplicated/total-4-edge signature with pre-flip inversions>0 in the implicated cells (hypothesis confirmed) â€” per the project's measurement-before-fixes rule, 4b does not start until this lands. 4b: diagnoseTopoQuality on REAL exports (the faithful e2e gate, not UV-only unit tests) â€” SFB@1 at AUTO uBias (B=3) nonMan 3->0; Voronoi nonMan 1->0; pre-flip inversion counter == 0 across all 20 styles at default AND the four dimension extremes; diagnoseFeatures featureDrop=0 maintained (re-planarize changes interior Steiner sets on every inserted-feature style â€” Hive, CelticKnot/Triquetra, Gyroid, Voronoi, SFB â€” so re-baseline those with the full protocol).
stages.effort: medium (2-4 days: repro harness + fix + re-measure)

stages.name: Stage 5 â€” Crest-aligned quality inside feature cells: in-metric flips + corrected-kernel interior Steiners
stages.what: The diagonal-crest sawtooth lives in cdt2d-FILLED feature cells, which Stages 2-3 never touch: the per-cell CDT runs in raw (u,t) with zero quality constraints (ConstrainedCellTriangulator:76-77), so triangles legally bridge a diagonal crest chord to the far cell corner and become 3D needles under sqrt(E)/sqrt(G)>>1. Two-part fix, both industry-standard (BAMG in-metric connectivity selection; BLSURF parameter-space insertion). Part 1 (connectivity only): post-CDT Lawson flips of INTERIOR, non-constraint, non-boundary edges under the FROZEN per-cell warp-composed efg â€” a constant metric is an affine pullback of Euclidean, so flips provably terminate and yield the constrained Delaunay triangulation in that metric, which maximizes the min metric (=true 3D) angle over all triangulations of the cell's PSLG. Part 2 (conditional, gated on Part 1's residual): crest-aligned interior Steiner points via the EXISTING refineCellInterior replay wiring (FCT:836-842 â€” boundary + constraints replayed unchanged, STEINER_MIN_EDGE_DIST=2e-6 guard) with a CORRECTED kernel: the ruled-out failure was the flat-3D-plane off-center + affine barycentric back-map, NOT the wiring or the contract. New kernel: read the local crest tangent directly from the cell's data.constraints chords (b-a â€” no new plumbing, verified available), offset candidates ALONG the metric-normal direction at metric-unit spacing in (u,t), evaluate positions analytically on the true surface, and ACCEPT each insertion only if the measured 3D min angle of the affected triangles improves (Bossen-Heckbert discipline) â€” so any metric-vs-3D mismatch can only no-op, never manufacture slivers like refineCellInterior did. Also handle the separate needle source the verified facts flag: dedupSide on NON-clean transition edges uses tol=1e-9 only (FCT:744), so near-coincident survivors there must be handled by the flip pass, not assumed merged.
stages.watertightArgument: Uses exactly the three moves the verified watertight contract permits inside a constrained cell: (a) flips touch only interior edges â€” the closed boundary loop is replayed as cdt2d constraints so perimeter connectivity is pinned, constraint (crest) chords are excluded from flipping so inserted features survive as mesh edges, and no vertex is added or moved; (b) interior Steiners keep >=2e-6 clearance from every side (>= 2x WELD_TAU, > one QSCALE quantum), so the global jitter-weld and quantized-dedup backstops cannot alias them onto shared vertices; (c) constraint endpoints on the boundary are never moved. Both adjacent cells' shared-edge vertex sets are bit-identical before and after â€” the fill is a per-cell interior decision by construction, the same argument that makes the existing PASS-A planarize Steiners safe.
stages.measuredGate: crestBandTriangleQuality ~ bulk level (no sub-15-degree clusters along inserted crests) on SFB@1, Hive, CelticKnot, CelticTriquetra, Gyroid, Voronoi, BasketWeave; visual confirmation via the min-angle tint probe (e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs) â€” no red on crests; remaining CDT-style sliver counts (Voronoi residual after Stage 3) -> 0; SFB@1 sub-15-degree residual at auto-B drops from 10.4% toward bulk; watertight vector (bnd/nonMan/orient = 0) maintained on all 20 + extremes; feature-dense build time <= 1.5x current.

stages.name: Stage 6 â€” SpiralRidges ceiling check + conditional re-route of helical crests to feature insertion
stages.what: Conditional stage, decided by Stage 2/5 measurement, because SpiralRidges has a PROVABLE in-cell limit: the post-warp cells are parallelograms with corner angle theta = atan(sqrt(G)/(s*sqrt(E))) (s = shearRate = turns/k; scale-invariant â€” squatting cells does NOT open the angle, which is exactly why axis-aligned uBias cannot square them), and a corner's angle is partitioned among its incident triangles, so NO triangulation â€” diagonal choice OR interior Steiners â€” can exceed it. Step 6a (instrument, always lands): compute theta per cell from the composed efg (one-line analytic addition to the Stage 2 instrumentation) and report the distribution alongside crestBandTriangleQuality and sliver counts for SpiralRidges. Expected: the 479 aspect>100 needles are NOT the ceiling (the ceiling forces low-but-bounded angles, not aspect-100 degeneracy) and die in Stage 2 via correct short-diagonal selection; the open question is whether the residual sub-15-degree population (best known 2.3% at forced B=2) sits above or below the crest-band gate. Step 6b (only if the ceiling binds below the gate): stop warping SpiralRidges â€” drop the chooseHelixGrid/applyHelixWarp route for this style and instead emit its helical crests as kind 'general-curve' polylines from the extractor (FeatureLineGraph already holds the exact analytic helices; extractSpiralRidges is the only helical-crease emitter, verified), so they are CDT-inserted as constrained diagonal edges in the UNWARPED domain and handled by the Stage 4 correctness + Stage 5 crest-aligned fill â€” the machinery that by then demonstrably handles diagonal crests on SFB/Gyroid-class styles. This removes the shear entirely (cells stay axis-aligned rectangles; the crest becomes real mesh edges), trading the warp's column-pinning elegance for the already-proven feature path.
stages.watertightArgument: 6a is observability-only. 6b uses ONLY the existing, registry-proven general-curve insertion contract: crest polyline points are snapped to cell edges and registered on grid-line registries so both neighbours derive bit-identical edge points; no new topology mechanism, no warp (the entire helix cap-rotation / seam-branch machinery at PEC:2443-2481 becomes a per-style no-op â€” strictly LESS post-assembly mutation). The style's watertightness then rests on the same PASS A/B + planarize + per-cell CDT argument as SFB/Hive â€” hardened by Stage 4's fail-loud inversion counter before this stage can land.
stages.measuredGate: 6a: theta-distribution report for SpiralRidges at auto-B and B=2; decision rule written down in advance: if post-Stage-2 sliver=0 AND crestBandTriangleQuality ~ bulk, the stage CLOSES as a no-op. 6b (if triggered): SpiralRidges sliver=0, bnd=0, nonMan=0, orient=0, featureDrop=0 at default + extremes; crest band shows no sub-15-degree clusters; silhouette visual (min-angle tint + profile render) shows no sawtooth; build time within envelope.
stages.effort: small for 6a (hours); medium for 6b if triggered (2-4 days)

stages.name: Stage 7 â€” Dimension-extremes hardening, real-export CI gate, cutover handshake
stages.what: (1) Run the full DONE matrix: 20 styles x {default, short-wide H40/R150, tall-narrow, high-flare, no-drain} through the faithful e2e export-fidelity path (real WebGPU exports â€” the watertight-gate finding establishes unit tests overstate seam cracks), recording sliver/bnd/nonMan/orient/featDrop, crestBandTriangleQuality, triangleQualityDistribution, and TRUE wallChordError with the decoupled dense reference (__pfReferenceDenseRes). (2) Diagnose-first the one KNOWN extremes defect: CelticKnot bnd=6 short-wide crack (cause unverified; suspects are the GATE A hasFeatures->0 deferral interplay at WA:133 and cornerSnap behavior at extreme wide/flat aspect â€” no fix proposed until a repro pins it, per the measurement rule). (3) Check GATE B's single-worst-point formula against the extremes: if extreme dims push maxURatio spikes that re-introduce over-biased slivers on styles Stage 2 cleaned, the named lever is metric GRADATION (percentile/log-ratio damping of B, the BAMG-standard guard against un-graded metric jumps) â€” scoped here, implemented only if the matrix demands it. (4) Land the CI gate: per-PR, a 4-6 style real-export subset asserting the zero-vector + crest-band + chord-error thresholds; nightly, the full matrix. (5) Hand the green matrix to the existing cutover plan's Phase 1 (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-export-pipeline-cutover-plan.md) â€” flag source, validationSummary, format, LOD/decimation, and the four divergent default sites are that plan's scope, NOT redesigned here; this design's deliverable to it is the measured all-zeros matrix plus the fact that the conforming gate's sliver-fatal strictness only becomes safe to default once the matrix is actually zero.
stages.watertightArgument: This stage adds measurement and CI, not meshing changes â€” the registry contract is untouched unless the extremes matrix forces a scoped fix, in which case that fix re-enters through the same per-stage discipline (interior-only or edge-local, with its own gate). The CelticKnot crack fix in particular must restore bnd=0 WITHIN the registry contract (edge-local shared-point functions), not via any post-hoc patch â€” the no-repair constraint is the acceptance criterion for whatever the diagnosis finds.
stages.measuredGate: The DONE vector, measured not claimed: all 20 styles x all 5 dimension sets report sliver=0, boundaryEdges=0, nonManifold=0, orientationMismatches=0, featuresDropped=0; crestBandTriangleQuality ~ bulk with visually confirmed no-red crest tint; TRUE chord error <= 0.03mm on smooth regions at the 'high' profile; 318+ unit tests green; default build 7-36s envelope not exceeded by >1.5x, feature-dense high <= ~1.5x current; CI gate red/green demonstrated by injecting one known-bad commit.
stages.effort: medium (3-5 days, dominated by matrix runtime and the CelticKnot diagnosis)

coverage.spiralRidgesShear: Addressed in two layers. Stage 2's warp-composed efg lets the certified shaped templates pick the true short diagonal of each post-warp parallelogram â€” expected to kill the 479 aspect>100 needles, which are mis-chosen diagonals/centroid fans under the unseen F cross-term, not the geometric ceiling. The residual is bounded by a PROVABLE parallelogram corner-angle ceiling theta = atan(sqrt(G)/(s*sqrt(E))) that no in-cell choice (diagonal or Steiner) can beat; Stage 6a instruments theta explicitly, and Stage 6b's conditional re-route (helical crests as inserted general-curve features in the unwarped domain, handled by Stage 4+5 feature machinery) is the escape if the ceiling binds below the crest-band gate. Forced B=2 (best known, 2.3% sub-15-degree) remains the documented containment if 6b is rejected on cost.
coverage.sfbCdtNonManifold: Addressed at root in Stage 4: the leading hypothesis (verified order-of-operations: planarizeConstraints runs in PASS A at FCT:634 BEFORE the PASS-B endpoint merges whose snap box is 2^B:1 anisotropic per FCT:263-264, so merges re-introduce interior-crossing constraints that cdt2d folds and the silent negative-area flip at CCT:84-86 converts into duplicated edges) is first CONFIRMED by a one-cell repro + pre-flip inversion counter (measurement-before-fixes), then fixed by re-planarizing the RESOLVED constraints in PASS B plus fail-loud inversion detection with a deterministic local fallback. Gate: SFB@1 at auto-B=3 nonMan 3->0 and Voronoi nonMan 1->0 on real exports. The one-line hasFeatures B-cap at WA:151 is held as containment-of-last-resort only, because it forfeits the proven B=3 serration gain and leaves the bug armed.
coverage.nineSliveredStyles: Addressed across Stages 2, 3, and 5 by routing: Stage 2 (shaped templates with composed efg) covers the plain-path members (ArtDeco, Crystalline, DragonScales, GeometricStar, BambooSegments, SpiralRidges) AND the feature-free inner walls of every CDT style (they share the outer uBias); Stage 3 covers the plain cells inside CDT outer walls (BasketWeave, CelticTriquetra, Voronoi); Stage 5 covers the feature cells themselves (Voronoi's residual + crest-adjacent needles). Caveat made explicit in the gates: the read-only agents disagree on which of the 9 route through the CDT path, so each stage gate first verifies per-style routing at ConformingWall.ts:447 before attributing improvements â€” per-style attribution is a measured output of Stages 2-3, not an assumption.
coverage.smoothBelowPrinterRes: Not changed by this design â€” already shipped (commit 9ca22fe: profile-driven maxSag, 'high' default, smooth-surface facets 0.021mm < ~0.03mm printer resolution) â€” and explicitly PROTECTED: the shaped-template gate leaves isotropic B=0 cells on the legacy path byte-for-byte (code-verified ~2:1 aspect crossover in cellAspect3D), Stage 2's gate pins wallChordError with the decoupled dense reference on smooth styles, and Stage 7's CI gate makes the chord-error threshold a permanent regression tripwire.
coverage.highReliefResiduals: Partially addressed, honestly conditional. Stage 2's per-cell in-metric template selection is the correct first lever for FourierBloom/Crystalline relief anisotropy (their sub-20-degree residuals are direction-misaligned elements, the exact defect class in-metric selection cures), and Crystalline's 2027 default-dims slivers are in the Stage 2 gate. But if a percent-below-20-degree residual persists after Stages 2+5, the named follow-up is damping GATE B's single-worst-point max formula (percentile/log-ratio gradation, the BAMG-standard guard, scoped in Stage 7 item 3) â€” deliberately NOT in the minimal sequence because it re-baselines globally and the measurement may prove it unnecessary. If neither moves the residual, this class is not fully addressed by this design and would need per-region anisotropy (a t-analogue of uExtra with 2-axis balance), a structural extension explicitly deferred with the documented ArtDeco-timeout history as the reason.
coverage.diagonalCrestSerration: Addressed in Stage 5 (the actual fix site â€” cdt2d-filled FEATURE cells, which the handoff's Step A provably cannot reach): frozen-per-cell-metric Lawson flips of interior non-constraint edges (terminating + max-min-metric-angle optimal because a constant metric is an affine pullback of Euclidean) eliminate triangles spanning across the crest, and conditional crest-aligned interior Steiners (tangent read from data.constraints chords, candidates chosen in (u,t) under efg, evaluated analytically on the true surface, accept-only-if-3D-min-angle-improves) provide the fine-perpendicular/coarse-along structure where flips alone are insufficient. Stage 4's correctness fix is a prerequisite (no point shaping a fill that can silently fold). Gate is the purpose-built instrument: crestBandTriangleQuality ~ bulk plus the visual min-angle tint showing no red along crests.

risks: Re-baseline blast radius of Stage 2 is the single largest risk: shapedTemplate fires on ALL leaves of every B>0 wall (most styles at default dims post-GATE-B) including CDT styles' inner walls, and vertex/triangle counts DROP on every shaped transition cell (legacy centroid omitted, k -> k-2 triangles) â€” every snapshot, count-based test, TRIS_PER_LEAF=2 budget calibration (ConformingWall.ts:181), and LOD heuristic shifts at once. Mitigation: Stage 1 lands first with production byte-identical proof, Stage 2 carries the full 20-style re-measure protocol as part of its definition of done, and budget-search accuracy is explicitly re-checked.

Warp-composition correctness is silent-failure-prone: the efg wrapper must reproduce the EXACT application guards (helix only when u-warp is identity; t-warp walls only; helix on BOTH walls; caps rigid) and forward gridResolution() (else metricStepsForSampler falls back to DEFAULT_H=1e-4, which the codebase itself documents as quantization-noise amplification); GpuSurfaceSampler.position's tolerance of the un-folded u that applyHelixWarp emits is UNVERIFIED and must be checked before composing. A mis-metered metric does not break watertightness but quietly optimizes the wrong objective exactly where the fix targets.

The Stage 4 root-cause story (planarize-before-merge re-crossing + silent winding flip) is a code-reading hypothesis, not yet reproduced â€” the design gates the fix behind a one-cell repro per the project's measurement rule; if the repro refutes it, Stage 4 reverts to diagnosis with the B=2 hasFeatures cap as interim containment (accepting SFB@1's 10.4% sub-15-degree quality cost at B=2).

Making the negative-area flip fail-loud may surface latent fold-overs in braid-heavy styles that currently pass topology checks by luck â€” budget triage time when the counter first runs across all 20 styles; this is a feature (defects become measurable) but a schedule risk.

Per-leaf efg is the leaf-CENTRE metric treated as constant; on feature-dense styles with sub-cell relief the centre sample can misrepresent edge regions â€” the same curved-surface error class that killed refineCellInterior. The failure mode here is bounded (suboptimal diagonal, never geometric corruption, and Stage 5 insertions are accept-only-if-improved), but gains must be measured via crestBandTriangleQuality, not assumed.

Agent reports conflict on which of the 9 slivered styles route through the CDT path vs the plain path (one says only Voronoi routes there; another lists BasketWeave/CelticTriquetra braids as feature-bearing) â€” per-style routing must be verified at ConformingWall.ts:447 during Stage 2/3 gating before attributing improvements, otherwise stages may be declared successful on the wrong evidence.

SpiralRidges may hit the provable parallelogram corner-angle ceiling below the crest-band gate, forcing Stage 6b's re-route â€” a material change to that style's meshing path (warp removal + general-curve insertion) whose quality then depends entirely on Stages 4+5 having landed well; the dependency ordering is deliberate but makes SpiralRidges potentially the last style to reach DONE.

CelticKnot short-wide bnd=6 crack has no verified root cause; Stage 7 budgets a diagnosis but the fix size is unknown â€” if it implicates the GATE A feature-deferral or cornerSnap scaling at extreme aspect, it could interact with Stage 4's tolerance changes and require a joint re-measure.

Perf: Stage 5's per-cell flips and accept/reject Steiner evaluations add work on feature-dense styles already at 60-236s high-fidelity; the gates carry an explicit <=1.5x envelope, and the Klincsek DP / flip passes are O(n^2-n^3) on polygons of size 4-12 so the expected cost is small, but the envelope must be measured per stage, not trusted.

Process risk: the hard constraint says clean-style default output stays byte-identical unless explicitly re-baselined with full re-measure â€” Stages 2, 3, 4, and 5 each trigger that protocol; skipping it on any 'small' change (e.g. Stage 4's tie-break pinning, which silently changes intra-cell constraint routing) would violate the constraint unnoticed.
whyThisOverAlternatives: The research survey is unambiguous: the quadtree + edge-local vertex registry + per-cell constrained CDT + analytic GPU evaluation IS the literature-standard architecture for watertight-by-construction parametric meshing (restricted-quadtree crack rules, edges-before-faces feature conformance, hardware-tessellation edge-invariance â€” all isomorphic to the registry contract), so every rip-and-replace alternative either forfeits a hard constraint the rebuild exists to guarantee or re-converges to what already exists. Dual contouring and field-aligned remeshers (Instant Meshes/QuadriFlow) put vertices off the analytic surface and require the forbidden validate-and-repair pass; OpenSubdiv swaps the true surface for a limit surface and re-imports the crest-alignment problem as cage design; GPU-tessellation patterns add nothing the registry doesn't already provide, and fractional modes deliberately manufacture the transition slivers being eliminated. The one principled alternative â€” BLSURF-style curve-first Riemannian advancing-front in the parameter domain â€” attacks the same root cause this design attacks, but at the cost of discarding the proven watertight core for months of advancing-front robustness engineering; it stays as the documented fallback, triggered only by a MEASURED ceiling (Stage 6a's theta instrument and Stage 5's crest-band residual are precisely those triggers). Meanwhile the codebase already contains, as verified dead code, the 80% version of the industry mechanism: in-metric max-min-angle connectivity selection (BAMG's unit-mesh principle, where in-metric angle = true 3D angle on a parametric surface) gated on a leaf.efg that production never populates, with firstFundamentalForm already written and pullback composition through warps (M = J^T J of the full chain) being standard practice. This design is therefore the smallest sequence that is simultaneously (a) watertight-safe at every step under the existing registry contract â€” every change is interior-only, edge-local, or observability-only, with the one genuinely unproven corner (earClip weak-convexity: the unguarded break at QT:181 and area-unchecked final-3 at QT:188-190, both verified first-hand) certified by exhaustive property test or replaced outright by Klincsek's exact DP BEFORE activation; (b) honest about provable limits â€” it names the parallelogram corner-angle ceiling for SpiralRidges and GATE B's worst-point formula as the residual-risk levers instead of promising in-cell fixes the geometry forbids, and it corrects the handoff in two evidence-forced ways (plain efg must never ship without warp composition because the u-/t-warps are also post-triangulation; Step A cannot touch CDT outer walls because triangulateQuadtreeWithFeatures never reads efg); and (c) measurement-gated per the project's own rules, with each stage independently landable, the suite green throughout, and the final deliverable being the measured all-zeros matrix the existing cutover plan's Phase 1 needs before the conforming gate's sliver-fatal strictness can safely become the default users hit.

title: Crest-Structured Conforming Export: Metric Foundation + Offset-Curve Ribbons Through the Existing Registry
thesis: Treat inserted crests as first-class meshing objects instead of obstacles the axis-aligned CDT trips over. The design lands in three layers, all inside the existing watertight-by-construction contract (every shared-cell-edge vertex is an interior-independent, edge-local function shared via the grid-line registry; no repair passes): (1) harden the constrained-CDT constraint topology (re-planarize after endpoint resolution; turn the silent negative-area flip into a detection signal) â€” this is both the SFB@1 non-manifold root-cause fix and the prerequisite for adding more constraint curves; (2) activate and harden the dead in-metric machinery (leaf.efg + max-min-3D-angle templates, warp-composed pullback metric) â€” this is what the 8 plain-path slivered styles actually need and what ribbons would never touch; (3) the centerpiece: along each inserted general-curve crest, build a thin structured ribbon as ADDITIONAL GLOBAL CONSTRAINT POLYLINES â€” left/right metric-offset curves at distance Î´ plus matched-station rungs â€” fed through the exact same clip/registry/planarize insertion machinery as the crests themselves. The band between crest and offset becomes a ladder of fine-perpendicular/coarse-along elements by construction (gmsh BoundaryLayer / LayTracks / Sculpt-pillowing lineage), and the offset curve IS the watertight transition boundary to the background quadtree, so no triangle ever spans across a crest. SpiralRidges, whose crest exists only via the post-triangulation helix shear, is fixed first by pullback metric and â€” if the provable sheared-corner-angle ceiling (cos Î¸ = s/âˆš(1+sÂ²)) leaves residual â€” by composing the shear into the sampling map so the lattice itself is crest-aligned (d'Azevedo), with positions arithmetically identical to today's warp.

stages.name: Stage 1 â€” Single-cell CDT repro + fold-over instrument (measurement first)
stages.what: Dev-gated diagnostics only, production byte-identical: (a) count pre-flip negative-area triangles in ConstrainedCellTriangulator BEFORE the silent [a,c,b] winding flip at :84-86 and surface the count per cell; (b) under a __pf dev flag, dump the boundary/interior/constraints of any cell with inversions>0 during an export; (c) a replay unit harness that feeds a dumped cell back through triangulateConstrainedCell. Run SFB@1 at auto-B (B=3) and verify the fold-over hypothesis: inversion cells co-located with the 3 duplicated/total=4 edges reported by diagnoseTopoQuality. If the count is 0, the hypothesis is refuted and Stage 2 is re-scoped BEFORE writing fix code (project rule: measurement before fixes).
stages.watertightArgument: Pure instrument: counters and dev-gated dumps alter no vertex, no constraint, no triangle. Production output is byte-identical by construction; the 20-style default byte-identity constraint is trivially satisfied.
stages.measuredGate: On SFB@1 auto-B3: pre-flip inversion count > 0 in at least one cell, and those cells spatially coincide with the duplicated-edge non-manifold locations from diagnoseTopoQuality (e2e export-fidelity, real export). Secondary: inversion count == 0 on all currently-topology-clean styles. 318 unit tests green; byte-identity spot-check on 3 styles.

stages.name: Stage 2 â€” CDT constraint-topology hardening (SFB non-manifold root fix; prerequisite for ribbons)
stages.what: Three changes in the feature-cell pipeline, in order of the verified order-of-operations bug (PASS-A planarize at FCT:634 runs BEFORE the PASS-B endpoint merges that can re-cross constraints): (1) re-run planarization (segSegCross + interior Steiner split) on the RESOLVED index-space cellConstraints in PASS B, between endpoint resolution (:796-813) and triangulateConstrainedCell (:822); (2) forbid dedupSide/interior-weld merges that join endpoints belonging to DIFFERENT feature lines unless the re-planarity check passes (the 8:1-anisotropic snap box at B=3, cornerSnapU=cornerSnap/2^B vs cornerSnapT=cornerSnap at :263-264, is the amplifier); (3) make Stage 1's pre-flip inversion count a per-cell error signal with a deterministic local fallback â€” retry the cell once without cross-line merges, then drop the newest interior constraint â€” never a silent flip. Pin resolve()'s order-dependent tie-break (:808-811) to deterministic min-qk. Explicitly NOT capping GATE B at B=2: that forfeits the measured serration gain and leaves the latent bug armed.
stages.watertightArgument: Re-planarization splits constraints only at strictly-interior crossing points â€” the identical cross-cell-safe argument the code already states for PASS-A planarize (FCT:201-241): Steiner points are interior with STEINER_MIN_EDGE_DIST=2e-6 clearance from every side, so the cell's boundary vertex set and all registry-shared points are untouched and both neighbours keep bit-identical shared-edge vertex sets. The merge restriction only PREVENTS vertex moves (a strict subset of today's merges). The fallback drops only interior constraints, never boundary points. No post-hoc repair anywhere.
stages.measuredGate: SFB@1 at auto-B (B=3): nonManifold 3â†’0, duplicated edges 4â†’0, bnd=0 (diagnoseTopoQuality on a real e2e export); inversion counter == 0 across all 20 styles at default; all currently-clean styles keep bnd/nonMan == 0; byte-identity expected on styles with no feature cells, explicit re-baseline for inserted-feature styles whose Steiner sets shift (Hive, CelticKnot, Gyroid, Voronoi, SFB).

stages.name: Stage 3 â€” Activate the metric: harden earClip, populate leaf.efg (warp-free styles), port templates to the feature path's plain cells
stages.what: (a) Brute-force property test of earClipMaxMinAngle over rectangle+mids weakly-convex polygons Ã— random anisotropic metrics (the polygons have collinear corner-mid-corner runs); guard the two verified unproven corners: area-check the final-3 emission (QT:188-190) and convert the no-ear break (QT:181) into a deterministic fallback to the legacy centroid fan for that cell. Optionally replace the greedy ear order with Klincsek's O(nÂ³) DP (nâ‰ˆ4-12 here) for the certified max-min-3D-angle optimum at negligible cost. (b) Retain the metric sampler as a field in PeriodicBalancedQuadtree and populate efg in leaves() ONLY (:555-565; never leafOfCell â€” hot neighbors() path) via firstFundamentalForm at cell centers; this stage gates efg to styles where ALL THREE post-triangulation warps are identity, so the metric is never wrong and warped styles stay byte-identical. (c) Export the Efg/metricLen2/shapedTemplate/earClip helpers and mirror the Tier-1b templates (shorter-3D-diagonal for 0-split, earClip for transition cells) into FeatureConformingTriangulator's hardcoded-legacy plain branch (:687-707), so the plain cells of feature walls and the always-feature-free INNER walls of CDT styles benefit too.
