stages.watertightArgument: Interior-only connectivity choice, per the contract the code itself states (QT:138-148): both template families consume the IDENTICAL registry-built boundary polygon (corners + edge-local mids), and earClip/Klincsek emit exclusively existing boundary indices â€” no vertexIndex calls, no moved or added shared points â€” so every shared edge keeps its exact vertex set and T-junction-freedom is preserved. The no-ear fallback inserts only an interior centroid (today's legacy behaviour). The warp-identity gate means warped styles are byte-identical this stage; the property test closes the weak-convexity crack hazard BEFORE the path can fire in production.
stages.measuredGate: Property test: 0 cracks (every boundary sub-edge covered) and 0 zero-area emissions over â‰¥1e5 randomized polygonÃ—metric cases. Then on real exports: ArtDeco sliver 2107â†’0 and Crystalline 2027â†’0 (triangleQualityDistribution + summarizeConformingValidation), watertight 20/20, warped styles and smooth B=0 styles byte-identical (hash check), explicit re-baseline with full re-measure for the styles that change.

stages.name: Stage 4 â€” Warp-composed pullback metric (helix + u-warp + t-warp) for the warped styles
stages.what: Build wrapper samplers position(u,t)=base(uWarpOrHelix(u,t), tWarp(t)) that mirror the EXACT application guards at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2418-2481 (helix only when the crease u-warp is identity; t-warp walls only; helix on BOTH walls; caps not needed â€” they bypass the quadtree), forwarding gridResolution() so metricStepsForSampler does not fall back to the noise-amplifying DEFAULT_H. Pass them as the efgSampler for warped styles through assembleWatertight â†’ buildConformingWall â†’ buildQuadtreeAtScale (new optional AssemblyWallOptions/ConformingWallOptions field). Sizing field stays on the plain sampler â€” only the connectivity metric changes. Unit-pin the pullback algebra: composed efg must equal the closed form E'=EÂ·Ï†'Â², F'=Ï†'(Fâˆ’sE), G'=Gâˆ’2sF+sÂ²E for a synthetic shear.
stages.watertightArgument: efg is consumed ONLY by shapedTemplate/earClip diagonal-and-ear selection â€” vertex positions, refinement footprints, registry keys, and boundary vertex sets never read it. The wrapper sampler is used solely for metric evaluation, never for positions or sizing, so the watertight machinery is bit-for-bit the machinery Stage 3 already proved; only interior connectivity differs on warped styles (explicit re-baseline).
stages.measuredGate: Sliver counts â†’ 0 target on the warp-pinned slivered styles: DragonScales 2683, BasketWeave 1584, CelticTriquetra 1221, GeometricStar 166, BambooSegments 291; SpiralRidges 479 â†’ measured drop with the corner-angle ceiling acknowledged (gate: sub-15Â° fraction â‰¤ the B=2 best of 2.3%; full fix is Stage 7); Gothic featDrop stays 0 and crestBandTriangleQuality does not regress on any warped style; pullback unit pin green.

stages.name: Stage 5 â€” Crest ribbons: metric-offset curves + rungs as additional global constraint polylines (the centerpiece)
stages.what: New module FeatureRibbon.ts. For each inserted general-curve FeatureLine: (1) compute LEFT/RIGHT offset polylines at metric distance Î´(s) along the efg-normal of the local chord tangent (tangent = bâˆ’a of the ordered polyline; normal rotated 90Â° in the metric), with Î´(s)=min(c1Â·local sizing-field edge length, c2Â·cross-crest normal-curvature radius, c3Â·half the metric distance to the nearest other feature line or braid crossing); simplify (Douglas-Peucker), trim offsets to clearance Ï around braid crossings (the junction pocket stays ordinary constrained CDT with Stage-2 planarization), taper Î´â†’0 over the last samples of open polylines (morphing crests, e.g. SFB petal-count morphs; the â‰¥0.85-span extractor filter means few of these at default), and wrap periodic u exactly as the crest polyline does. (2) Optionally emit matched-station RUNG segments crest_kâ†’offset_k at coarse along-metric spacing, forcing explicit ladder quad-pairs; land offsets first behind __pfCrestRibbon, measure, add rungs only if offsets alone leave residual. (3) Tag ribbon lines as synthetic so featureDrop coverage still measures REAL features only; append them to the generalCurves list feeding outerFeatureLines at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2329-2331 â€” they then flow through the unmodified collectSegments â†’ Liang-Barsky clip â†’ edge-crossing registry â†’ snap â†’ planarize pipeline. Result: the band beside every crest is filled with fine-perpendicular/coarse-along elements by construction, and background cells CDT against the near-parallel offset (at â‰ˆ a fraction of local cell size) instead of spanning across the crest â€” the sawtooth mechanism is removed at the source.
stages.watertightArgument: Ribbons introduce NO new topology mechanism: offsets and rungs are global constraint polylines inserted through the exact existing contract â€” every vertex they produce on a shared cell edge is the intersection of (global segment Ã— shared edge LINE), computed by the same edge-local formula on both sides and unified via the grid-line registry (FCT:147-177, regH/regV :514-522), so neighbours derive bit-identical points; interior crossings (rungÃ—offset, offsetÃ—crest near junctions) are split by planarization on RESOLVED constraints (Stage 2) into strictly-interior Steiner points; nothing is ever moved post-hoc. Ribbon/grid transitions are watertight because the offset curve is itself just another inserted curve â€” the 'transition' is an ordinary feature cell. The periodic seam is covered by the registry's existing u=1â†’u=0 index remap, identical to crest seam crossings. The Î´ floor (â‰« WELD_TAU/QSCALE quantum and â‰¥ snap boxes) prevents near-duplicate-point pathologies; the c3 clearance term prevents offset self-intersection and cross-feature collisions.
stages.measuredGate: crestBandTriangleQuality on SFB@1, HexagonalHive, CelticKnot, Gyroid, Voronoi: sub-15Â° clusters in the crest band â†’ 0 and band min-angle distribution â‰ˆ bulk level; visual min-angle tint (e2e/_crest_stage0.cjs) shows no red along any inserted crest; Voronoi sliver 2076â†’0 with nonMan staying 0; featureDrop(real)=0; watertight 20/20; feature-dense build time â‰¤ ~1.5Ã— current (60-236s budget).

stages.name: Stage 6 â€” Per-cell metric-stretched constrained CDT (feature-cell fill mop-up)
stages.what: Before calling cdt2d, affinely transform the cell's (u,t) point coordinates by M^(1/2) of the FROZEN cell-center composed efg (constant per cell), run cdt2d in stretched coordinates, and use the returned indices unchanged. A constant metric is an affine pullback of Euclidean, so the in-cell result is the constrained Delaunay triangulation IN THE METRIC â€” max-min metric angle (= true 3D angle) optimal among all triangulations of that cell's points/constraints. Critically, dedupSide/posTol/withinSnap and all cross-cell merge decisions stay in RAW coordinates: per-cell metrics differ between neighbours, so any metric-dependent merge would be asymmetric (crack hazard) â€” only the interior fill choice is stretched. This cleans junction pockets, trimmed-ribbon ends, and any feature cell ribbons do not cover.
stages.watertightArgument: The vertex SET (boundary + interior) and every constraint segment are unchanged â€” the stretch only changes WHICH interior diagonals cdt2d selects. The closed boundary loop is still replayed as cdt2d constraints, so all collinear perimeter vertices are retained; which boundary points exist or merge is decided in raw coordinates exactly as today, so both neighbours keep bit-identical shared-edge vertex sets. Affine maps preserve straightness and incidence, so constraint conformance survives the stretch.
stages.measuredGate: Residual feature-cell slivers (junction pockets, non-ribbon feature cells) â†’ 0 in triangleQualityDistribution; SFB@1 sub-15Â° residual (10.4% at forced B=2; current value at auto-B after Stage 2) â†’ â‰¤ bulk level; diagnoseTopoQuality identical before/after (bnd/nonMan/orientation unchanged â€” connectivity-only change must not move topology).

stages.name: Stage 7 â€” SpiralRidges lattice alignment: compose the helix shear into the sampling map (gated on Stage 4 residual)
stages.what: Triggered ONLY if Stage 4 leaves SpiralRidges above the sliver gate â€” the provable ceiling: a cell sheared to corner angle Î¸ (cos Î¸ = s/âˆš(1+sÂ²)) admits no triangulation with min angle > Î¸, with or without interior Steiner points, so diagonal selection cannot finish the job at high shear. Fix per d'Azevedo lattice alignment: replace the post-triangulation applyHelixWarp vertex remap (PEC:2466-2481) with a COMPOSED SAMPLER used for sizing + metric + final evaluation â€” lattice coordinates (a,b) sample base(Ï†0(a) âˆ’ shearRateÂ·b + offset, b). Final vertex positions are arithmetically identical to today (the same composition the warp performs, applied before instead of after), but the quadtree's lattice-vertical direction now follows the helix, so cells are rectangles in 3D and the crest columns remain lattice-vertical â€” the existing vertical-crease pin/refine machinery (chooseCreaseGrid anchors, biasFreeU) applies unchanged; buildCreaseRefineLines' helix source resolution simplifies. Caps and rim share ring vertex indices with the walls, so they follow automatically.
stages.watertightArgument: Only the sampler changes â€” the entire topology machinery is untouched: every vertex is still a dyadic lattice point deduped via QSCALE-quantized registry keys, mid-vertex decisions remain edge-local probes of the shared leaf set, 2:1 balance and the seam index-remap are unchanged, and positions are exact analytic evaluations of the same composed map the post-warp computes today (GpuSurfaceSampler.position already wraps u internally, covering the unfolded helix u). Watertightness is therefore inherited from the unchanged construction; refinement footprints change (sizing sees the composed surface) â†’ explicit single-style re-baseline.
stages.measuredGate: SpiralRidges: sliver 479â†’0, sub-15Â° fraction â‰¤ bulk, crestBandTriangleQuality along the helical crests â‰ˆ bulk, no red in the min-angle tint; TRUE chord error (decoupled __pfReferenceDenseRes reference) â‰¤ 0.03mm maintained; byte-diff confirmed limited to SpiralRidges; all other styles hash-identical.

stages.name: Stage 8 â€” Dimension-extremes matrix, gradation guard, and cutover slot-in
stages.what: Run the full Definition-of-Done matrix â€” 20 styles Ã— {default, short-wide H40/R150, tall-narrow, high-flare, no-drain} â€” through the faithful e2e export-fidelity gate (real WebGPU exports; unit tests overstate seam cracks), recording sliver/bnd/nonMan/orientation/featDrop/crestBandTriangleQuality/decoupled chord error/build time, and add a CI-runnable subset (per the existing cutover plan's CI phase). If extremes expose grading-transition slivers (the documented ArtDeco-class mechanism: global B over-corrects t-dominant bands), add a BAMG-style gradation bound â€” clamp the log-ratio of adjacent sizing targets â€” in MetricSizingField.gradeLipschitz (scalar, affects only refinement decisions). Hand the green matrix to Phase 1 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-export-pipeline-cutover-plan.md; flag source, validationSummary, format fix, LOD, and CI plumbing stay that plan's scope and are not redesigned here.
stages.watertightArgument: Gradation alters only cell-size refinement decisions inside the sizing field â€” vertex derivation rules (dyadic lattice + registry + edge-local probes) are untouched, so watertightness is unaffected; everything else in this stage is measurement.
stages.measuredGate: The DoD numbers themselves, all measured: every style Ã— every dimension extreme reports sliver=0, boundaryEdges=0, nonManifold=0, orientationMismatches=0, featuresDropped=0; crest band has no sub-15Â° clusters (instrument + visual tint); decoupled chord error â‰¤ ~0.03mm on smooth regions; build time â‰¤ ~1.5Ã— today's envelope; 318+ unit tests green.

coverage.spiralRidgesShear: Addressed in two gated steps. Stage 4 composes the helix shear Jacobian into efg (pullback E'=EÏ†'Â², F'=Ï†'(Fâˆ’sE), G'=Gâˆ’2sF+sÂ²E) so the max-min-angle templates see the parallelogram skew â€” bounded upside, gated at â‰¤ the B=2 best (2.3% sub-15Â°), because diagonal choice cannot beat the sheared-corner ceiling cos Î¸ = s/âˆš(1+sÂ²) with immutable cell-boundary vertices. Stage 7 is the full fix: compose the shear into the sampling map itself (positions arithmetically identical to today's post-warp), making the lattice crest-aligned so cells are 3D rectangles â€” gate sliver 479â†’0. The feature-first alternative (insert helical crests as general curves + ribbons, drop the warp) was considered and rejected: the warp's column pinning is exact and cheaper, and Stage 7 achieves alignment without giving it up.

risks: Re-baseline blast radius: Stage 3/4 efg activation flips ALL leaves of every B>0 wall (including inner walls of CDT styles) onto shaped templates, and shaped transition cells drop the legacy centroid (vertex/triangle counts shrink) â€” the byte-identity constraint is intentionally violated for those styles and requires the full re-measure protocol, count-based tests/budget calibration (TRIS_PER_LEAF=2) re-checked, and the identity-warp gate verified so warp-pinned styles do not change before Stage 4.

earClipMaxMinAngle's two weak-convexity corners (unguarded final-3 emission, no-ear break leaving uncovered boundary sub-edges = a real crack) are reachable in principle on the collinear corner-mid-corner polygons it will receive; Stage 3's property test and guards are a hard prerequisite â€” if the property test finds reachable cracks the greedy must be replaced (Klincsek DP), not patched.

The Stage 2 root-cause is still a code-reading hypothesis until Stage 1 reproduces it; if the single-cell repro shows zero pre-flip inversions, the duplicated-edge mechanism is elsewhere (e.g. cross-cell dedup asymmetry) and Stage 2 must be re-scoped before any fix lands (project rule: measurement before fixes).

Ribbon offset geometry is the classic offset-curve tar pit: cusps/self-intersection where crest curvature radius < Î´, collisions between offsets of adjacent crests in feature-dense styles (Voronoi cell walls are close), and Î´ interacting with cornerSnap/WELD_TAU/QSCALE quanta â€” mitigated by the c2/c3 clamps, a hard Î´ floor above all snap/weld tolerances, and trimming at junctions, but Voronoi at dimension extremes is the stress test and may force per-style Î´ tuning.

Ribbons add constraint segments and edge crossings to every crest-adjacent cell: planarization is O(kÂ²) per cell and feature-dense builds already run 60-236s at high fidelity â€” the Stage 5 gate includes a â‰¤1.5Ã— build-time budget, and rungs (the biggest constraint multiplier) are deliberately deferred behind a measured residual.

Stage 4's wrapper must reproduce the exact warp application guards (helix XOR u-warp, t-warp walls-only, both walls) â€” a silent mismatch mis-meters the metric precisely in the crest neighbourhoods being fixed, potentially worsening local serration while bulk improves; the closed-form pullback unit pin and per-style crestBand gates are the defense.

Stage 7 changes SpiralRidges refinement footprints (sizing sees the composed surface), so its triangle layout re-baselines entirely; the t-clamping of metric finite-difference steps and the pinned boundary rings at t=0/1 must be re-verified under the composed map, and GpuSurfaceSampler's internal u-wrap must be confirmed for unfolded helix u before relying on it.

Cutover-gate asymmetry (inherited, not created here): the conforming validation gate hard-fails on sliverCount>0 while legacy ships defects as advisory â€” landing Stages 2-4 before any default flag flip is mandatory ordering, otherwise the stricter gate turns today's 9-style sliver carriers into export-blocking errors for users; the existing cutover plan's Phase 1 owns this sequencing.

Per-leaf efg is the cell-CENTER metric treated as constant; on sub-cell relief the center sample can misrepresent edges (the error class that killed refineCellInterior) â€” the failure mode here is only 'suboptimal diagonal' (no positions are manufactured), but every stage gate measures crestBandTriangleQuality/sliver counts rather than assuming the in-metric choice wins.
whyThisOverAlternatives: Three honest comparisons. (1) Versus the handoff's metric-only plan (Steps A/B/C: efg + warp composition + CDT fix): this design CONTAINS it as Stages 1-4 and 6 â€” measurement says most of the 9-style sliver mass is plain-path anisotropy that only the metric templates can fix, so they come first â€” but metric connectivity selection has a provable ceiling: it cannot move cell-boundary vertices, so crest-crossed cells with boundary-pinned acute geometry (the diagonal-crest sawtooth, the Voronoi curve-needle bands, high-shear SpiralRidges) keep a residual the handoff has no answer for. The ribbon stage is that answer, and it is the industry-consensus one â€” every production lineage surveyed (gmsh BoundaryLayer/LayTracks, Sandia Sculpt pillowing, Netgen/OCC curve-first advancing front) puts structured fine-perpendicular/coarse-along elements along feature curves rather than CDT-filling axis-aligned cells against a diagonal constraint and accepting the angles. (2) Versus rip-and-replace alternatives (BLSURF-style global curve-first advancing front, dual contouring, subdivision surfaces, field-aligned remeshing): all either forfeit a hard constraint the rebuild exists to guarantee (off-surface vertices, mandatory repair passes, a limit surface that is not the analytic model) or re-derive what already exists (the registry IS the literature's crack-free shared-edge rule). Critically, ribbons capture the curve-first lineage's actual benefit â€” crest-aligned anisotropic elements â€” at a fraction of its cost, because they are expressed as ordinary global constraint polylines flowing through the proven clip/registry/planarize machinery: no new topology mechanism, no advancing-front robustness engineering, watertightness inherited rather than re-argued. (3) Versus doing ribbons FIRST (the naive reading of this angle): the ordering here is deliberate â€” ribbons multiply constraint density inside exactly the cells where the CDT's planarize-before-merge bug lives, so Stage 2 must precede them; and ribbons do nothing for 8 of the 9 slivered styles, whose defects are plain-path metric problems, so Stages 3-4 must carry that load or the design would ship the centerpiece while missing most of the measured defect mass. Every stage lands independently with the suite green, each has a single instrumented number that must move, and the whole sequence slots into Phase 1 of the existing cutover plan without redesigning its integration plumbing.
